% This is generated by ESQL's MvLikeTests. Do not edit it. See docs/reference/query-languages/esql/README.md for how to regenerate it.

### MV LIKE
Returns `true` when any value yielded by `field` matches `pattern`, using the same wildcard syntax as [`LIKE`](https://www.elastic.co/docs/reference/query-languages/esql/functions-operators/operators#esql-like-operator). Unlike `LIKE`, which is a single-value scalar, this reduces over every value of a multivalue field. A null or empty `field` returns `false`.

```esql
ROW names = ["Anna", "Bob", "Carl"]
| EVAL any_starts_with_a = mv_like(names, "A*")
```
